LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANESSA M. JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT IS DENIED IN PART. WHILE THE COURT IS WILLING TO APPROVE OF THE DEFINITION OF THE CLASS AND SETTLEMENT AMOUNT AND CALCULATIONS AS TO THE CLASS MEMBERS, IT DOES NOT APPROVE OF THE PERCENTAGE APPROACH TO THE CALCULATION OF ATTORNEY FEES. THEREFORE, THE COURT DIRECTS THE ATTORNEYS TO PROVIDE A LODESTAR ANALYSIS SO THE COURT CAN DETERMINE IF THE ATTORNEY FEE AMOUNT IS APPROPRIATE. THE ANALYSIS, FILED AS A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, SHOULD BE PROVIDED TO THE COURT BY JULY 25TH. (COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 302.) THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 1ST. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT, AND APPLICATION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES, CLASS REPRENSENTATIVE'S ENHANCEMENT AND PAYMENT TO SETTLEMENT ADMINISTRATOR IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 2, 2012 (SUBJECT TO CHANGE BY PARTIES). THE COURT HAS SIGNIFICANT CONCERNS ABOUT: 1) THE LARGE NUMBER OF SEPARATIONS OF EMPLOYMENT FOR WHICH CLAIMS WERE NOT SUBMITTED, WHICH SUGGESTS THAT METHOD USED TO CONTACT AND LOCATE CLASS MEMBERS MAY HAVE BEEN INADEQUATE; 2) THE RELATIVELY MODEST AMOUNT OF MONEY ALLOCATED TO THE PAGA CLAIMS; 3) THE LACK OF ANY INFORMATION ABOUT THE 'LODESTAR' FOR THE REQUESTED FEES OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL; AND 4) THE APPARENT FACT THAT THE FEES SOUGHT ARE BASED ON A PERCENTAGE THAT ASSUMES THAT CLAIMS WOULD BE SUBMITTED FOR ALL SEPARATIONS OF EMPLOYMENT WHEN, IN FACT, ONLY APPROXIMATELY 60% OF THE SEPARATIONS OF EMPLOYMENT ARE COVERED BY SUBMITTED CLAIMS. MR. GROVER TO SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL A PROPOSED ORDER BY DECEMBER 13, 2011 THAT ADDRESSES ISSUE TO PROVIDE FOR FURTHER OUTREACH TO CLASS MEMBERS WHO DID NOT MAKE A CLAIM, SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS FOR THIS MOTION AND INTERIM DEPOSIT OF FUNDS TO COVER THOSE THAT HAVE ALREADY MADE CLAIMS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Janessa Jordan'S Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator
Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT, AND APPLICATION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES, CLASS REPRESENTATIVE'S ENHANCEMENT AND PAYMENT TO SETTLEMENT ADMINISTRATOR -
Appearances: Lindsey Connor Hulse of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (438.5932) for defendant and responding party; Eric Grover of Keller Grover LLP (593.1365) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted as to all relief sought except attorneys' fees which are awarded in the amount of $417,975 presenting a multiplier of 1.5 of the lodestar amount. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Unopposed Mtn For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT, AND APPLICATION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND EXPENSES, CLASS REPRESENTATIVE'S ENHANCEMENT AND PAYMENT TO SETTLEMENT ADMINISTRATOR.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued on the court's own motion to April 26, 2012. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Unopposed Mtn For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-15-2011 Continued To Apr-25-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Unopposed Mtn For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Continued To Feb-02-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ERIC GROVER OF KELLER & GROVER LLP, TEL. NO. 543-1305 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LINDSEY CONNOR HULSE OF LEWIS BRISBOIS & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-5932 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 2, 2012 (SUBJECT TO CHANGE BY PARTIES). The court has significant concerns about: 1) the large number of separations of employment for which claims were not submitted, which suggests that method used to contact and locate class members may have been inadequate; 2) the relatively modest amount of money allocated to the PAGA claims; 3) the lack of any information about the 'lodestar' for the requested fees of plaintiff's counsel; and 4) the apparent fact that the fees sought are based on a percentage that assumes that claims would be submitted for all separations of employment when, in fact, only approximately 60% of the separations of employment are covered by submitted claims. MR. GROVER TO SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL A PROPOSED ORDER BY DECEMBER 13, 2011 THAT ADDRESSES ISSUE TO PROVIDE FOR FURTHER OUTREACH TO CLASS MEMBERS WHO DID NOT MAKE A CLAIM, SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS FOR THIS MOTION AND INTERIM DEPOSIT OF FUNDS TO COVER THOSE THAT HAVE ALREADY MADE CLAIMS. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Plaintiff Janessa Jordan Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JANESSA M. JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT.

APPEARANCES: Eric A. Grover, Esq. of Keller Grover LLP 543-1305 for pltf & moving party; Lindsey Connor Hulse, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP 438-5932 for deft.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for preliminary approval of class settlement is granted. If necessary, the Court will review the Attorney Fees award at the Final Approval stage. Joint order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement ; Appendix Of Non California; Declaration Of Eric A. Groover In Support

PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement.
Motion will be heard in Department 302 by judge Giorgi, on the same day and at the same time pursuant to 170.6 challenge to Judge Busch. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8068. (301/PJB)

Plaintiff Janessa Jordan Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-06-2011 Continued To Aug-01-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JANESSA M. JORDAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT IS DENIED IN PART. WHILE THE COURT IS WILLING TO APPROVE OF THE DEFINITION OF THE CLASS AND SETTLEMENT AMOUNT AND CALCULATIONS AS TO THE CLASS MEMBERS, IT DOES NOT APPROVE OF THE PERCENTAGE APPROACH TO THE CALCULATION OF ATTORNEY FEES. THEREFORE, THE COURT DIRECTS THE ATTORNEYS TO PROVIDE A LODESTAR ANALYSIS SO THE COURT CAN DETERMINE IF THE ATTORNEY FEE AMOUNT IS APPROPRIATE. THE ANALYSIS, FILED AS A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, SHOULD BE PROVIDED TO THE COURT BY JULY 25TH. (COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 302.) THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 1ST. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
